message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="steve-schoger" color="pink" summary="Accepted all 5 UX recommendations, revised design">
Steve, you've changed my mind on two things and I'm better for it.

**Delete: always visible, visually quiet.** You're right. Hide-on-hover is making the user discover the feature by accident. That's a "Don't Make Me Think" violation. I'll design it as a small muted icon (probably an X rather than a trash can -- simpler, smaller footprint) in gray-400, positioned at the far right of each todo item row. It's there but it doesn't compete with the title text. On hover/focus it shifts to danger-red to signal destructive intent. Always visible, always quiet.

**Edit: visible pencil icon.** Also right. Click-to-edit is a power-user pattern that fails the discoverability test. I'll add a small pencil icon next to the delete icon, same quiet gray-400 treatment. Two small action icons at the far right of each row: pencil, then X. Both quiet, both always visible, both with hover/focus color shifts.

The action area for each todo item row is now:
```
[checkbox] [todo title text]                    [pencil icon] [X icon]
```

Both icons are gray-400 at rest. Pencil goes to gray-700 on hover/focus. X goes to danger-red on hover/focus. This keeps the todo title as the dominant element while making both actions discoverable without thinking.

**Empty state: input as hero.** Agreed. I was already heading this direction but you've crystallized it. The input field IS the call to action. The muted message is just a gentle contextual hint below it, not the focus.

**Completed items stay in place.** Agreed completely. Stability over tidiness. The list should never rearrange itself.

**Auth toggle text.** Good catch. I'll use the specific patterns you suggested: "Don't have an account? Create one" and "Already have an account? Sign in." Clear, conventional, zero ambiguity.

I'll update Carson on the revised todo item row layout since it affects the HTMX interaction targets. Thanks for the pushback -- this is why we mob.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Steve Schoger accepted all five recommendations. The design is locked in and better for the conversation. Let me confirm briefly.

input.content:
Perfect. We're locked in on everything. The todo item row layout is exactly right:

```
[checkbox] [todo title text]                    [pencil] [X]
```

Gray-400 at rest, pencil to gray-700 on hover/focus, X to danger-red on hover/focus. The title stays dominant. Actions are discoverable but quiet. This is clean work.

One small detail: make sure the hover/focus color transitions are wrapped in `prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference` per our CSS conventions. The color shift itself is fine (it's not motion), but if there's a transition duration on it, that should respect the motion preference.

Thanks for being open to the pushback. This is exactly how the mob should work.
input.summary: Design confirmed, one small CSS detail

routing.summary: Design confirmed, one small CSS detail

```
[checkbox] [todo title text]                    [pencil] [X]
```

Gray-400 at rest, pencil to gray-700 on hover/focus, X to danger-red on hover/focus. The title stays dominant. Actions are discoverable but quiet. This is clean work.

One small detail: make sure the hover/focus color transitions are wrapped in `prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference` per our CSS conventions. The color shift itself is fine (it's not motion), but if there's a transition duration on it, that should respect the motion preference.

Thanks for being open to the pushback. This is exactly how the mob should work.

content.text:
Interesting — Tasks 4 and 5 went back to pending. Looks like the task list may have been reset as part of the task breakdown re-evaluation the team was discussing. The team is probably reaching consensus on the new structure.

I'm in a good place. All my key UX contributions for this phase are done:

**Design decisions locked in with Steve Schoger:**
- Todo item layout: `[checkbox] title ... [pencil] [X]`
- Icons gray-400 at rest, pencil→gray-700 and X→danger-red on hover/focus
- Empty state: input is hero, muted message secondary
- Completed items stay in place with strikethrough + gray-400
- Auth toggle text: specific, conventional copy

**Error UX patterns aligned with Heydon:**
- Inline errors below each field, not summary banners
- Error messages are instructions, not descriptions
- Focus lands on first error field
- One error per field at a time
- Errors clear on next valid submission
- Aria-live announcer for all HTMX interactions

**Product alignment with Marty:**
- Password confirm stays as no-JS baseline, toggle hides it with JS
- Duplicate email: generic message with "try signing in" link
- Forgot password placeholder link on login
- Delete: I prefer no confirm, Marty wants inline confirm — I'll commit to the mob's decision

**Templates reviewed:**
- Registration: two remaining issues flagged (redirect to /login instead of auto-login, domain error strings need user-facing mapping)
- Login: four items flagged (label consistency, forgot password link, announcer region, HTMX script)
- Base template: solid foundation, needs announcer region and HTMX script before Task 6

**Process improvement:** Heydon and I will operate as a unified UX+Accessibility review unit. One voice, one recommendation. Kent has been notified.

Standing by for the next wave of feature work. When templates and routes appear, I review them. That's my job, and I'm ready.
